image_build_notes: RESOLVED. Docker engine came up on its own after the user re-enabled/fixed whatever nested-virtualization issue blocked it previously (confirmed working via `docker info` showing Kernel Version 6.18.33.2-microsoft-standard-WSL2). Hit one real bug during build: `npm ci` triggers npm's known 'Exit handler never called!' error under this VM's very slow/flaky registry network — it can print the error but exit 0, leaving node_modules incomplete (missing vite binary) so the later `npm run build` failed with 'vite: not found'. Fixed in Dockerfile by (1) `npm config set progress false` (avoids the stdout-pipe race that triggers the npm bug), (2) generous fetch-retries/timeout config, (3) a shell retry loop (up to 3 attempts) that removes node_modules and re-runs npm ci until a marker file (node_modules/.bin/vite for frontend, node_modules/sql.js dir for backend) actually exists, hard-failing the build if all 3 attempts fail. This network is extremely slow — both npm ci steps took ~47 minutes each on the successful attempt, and a single 28MB base-image layer once took 500+ seconds. Build + smoke test (frontend at :9080, /api/health through nginx) passed. Also independently verified the FINAL standalone requirement: `docker run --rm -p 9080:9080 -p 8090:8090 meera-insights:final` with NO volume mount — both endpoints responded and a full POST /api/analyze round-trip worked correctly through the nginx proxy.
code_zip_note: The plugin's make_code_zip.ps1 only excludes top-level node_modules; this repo has backend/node_modules and frontend/node_modules nested one level down, so the first run bundled them in (24MB, node_modules leaked). Rebuilt manually with a path-segment-aware exclusion filter (checks every path part, not just parts[0]). Final zip: 40 files, ~135KB, verified clean of node_modules/.git/.env/*.db.
registry_push_blocker: Participant does not yet have the organizer-provided proxy registry token needed for `docker login`. Everything else for the push is ready: local image meera-insights:final is built and smoke-tested (both standalone and with data volume), team_id is derived from the Meesho email as 'peddula-goud'. Next action: once the participant has the token, run push_to_proxy_registry.ps1 with -Token <token> -LocalImage meera-insights:final -User peddula.goud@meesho.com (or set HACKATHON_PROXY_TOKEN env var). Do not invent or guess the token.
next_action: obtain proxy registry token from buildathon organizer, then push
docker_desktop_env_issue: 2026-07-02: Docker Desktop backend crash-looped on every launch with 'initializing Inference manager: listening on unix://.../AppData/Local/Docker/run/dockerInference: remove ...: The file cannot be accessed by the system.' Root cause: three orphaned AF_UNIX socket reparse points in %LOCALAPPDATA%\Docker\run\ (dockerEthernetVfkit, dockerInference, userAnalyticsOtlpHttp.sock) stuck at the OS/driver level — PowerShell Remove-Item, cmd /c del, and even fsutil reparsepoint query all failed on them with the identical error, and Docker's own 'Reset to factory defaults' (already tried by participant earlier same day) did not clear them since it only resets Docker config, not this OS-level socket state. This is NOT a project code bug. Fix: reboot Windows (releases the stuck AFD.sys-held reparse points), then relaunch Docker Desktop normally. Participant chose to reboot themselves. Next session: after reboot, just verify `docker info` succeeds and re-run the single-image smoke test — no code changes needed.
